text: The request surprises me at first, echoing awkwardness and vulnerability. Here, amidst bright utilitarian corridors and the colorful hum of intellectual display, the question seems at once both out of place and profoundly human. I feel a quiet awareness of the spaces we occupy—of intimacy, privacy, and the boundaries we gently press against in seeking connection and safety. I wonder what led to this moment and how openness, even in awkwardness, shapes the fabric of our shared existence.
